page: Amazon Web Services m6g.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by the custom 64-core AWS Graviton processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture with Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz, this server provides 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It features a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, lacks local instance storage, and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory efficiency for cached operations. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth at 3792.04 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, reaching 74.25 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 48.58 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Large-model LLM inference text generation also registers as poor, reflecting limitations in heavy sequential or single-threaded execution paths.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture offers direct access to the physical ARMv8 hardware, eliminating hypervisor overhead and maximizing resource density for multi-threaded workloads. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server type highly effective for containerized microservices, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and distributed caching systems like Redis. It is less optimal for workloads requiring high single-core throughput, local NVMe storage, or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 architecture. Running at 2.5 GHz, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. Conversely, single-core execution and LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the weak to poor ranges. This bare-metal configuration is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving, though it is less suited for single-threaded tasks or local storage-heavy databases.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server with 64 dedicated arm64 Graviton cores, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.metal is a 64-core arm64 bare-metal server with 256 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core arm64 bare-metal server with balanced memory density, optimized for highly concurrent multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical arm64 cores on AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 architecture
256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing a balanced 1:4 core-to-memory ratio
Strong multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
High-throughput capabilities for Redis operations and static web serving
Tradeoffs include weak single-core execution and lack of local storage
Best suited for multi-threaded software compilation, caching, and web serving
